source: EXP-303 (5 verified patches) + WIN-013.c5 H4 TLDR fix

patches.find: pending_independent_verification from the outset, with an honest caveat that independent reproduction is required. We credit both moves.
patches.replace: pending_independent_verification from the outset. We credit both moves.
patches.rationale: EXP-303 PATCH-303-A: ISS-1221 finding — trailing clause restates content already in the directly-preceding verbatim quote. Remove duplication.

patches.find: one of at least three parallel acknowledge-without-fix moves shipped inside a 24-hour window on 2026-04-17
patches.replace: one of three parallel acknowledge-without-fix moves shipped in commit 6e06efb (2026-04-17)
patches.rationale: EXP-303 PATCH-303-B: Joint fix for ISS-1261 (drop 'at least') and ISS-1223 WIN-062 leg (pin to commit SHA 6e06efb). Precision-parity with WIN-058.

patches.find: This is one of at least three acknowledge-without-fix moves the dome author shipped on 2026-04-17
patches.replace: This is one of three acknowledge-without-fix moves the dome author shipped in commit 6e06efb (2026-04-17)
patches.rationale: EXP-303 PATCH-303-C: ISS-1223 WIN-058b leg — pin to commit SHA 6e06efb, drop 'at least' hedge. Cross-WIN framing parity.

patches.find: reflecting rounding in Tesla's original estimate rather than a measurement discrepancy. The ~1.57c phase velocity is genuine physics
patches.replace: reflecting rounding in Tesla's original estimate rather than a measurement discrepancy. Note also that Tesla's own ratio is 471,240 km/s ÷ 299,792 km/s ≈ 1.5719c, not the dome's stated 1.574c — a small but visible ~0.13% offset that is what you get if you re-express the round-trip distance against the fitted disc_diameter (~40,030 km, the dome's 2 × disc_radius 20,015 km) instead of Tesla's published velocity: 40,030 / 0.08484 / c ≈ 1.5738c, which the dome rounds to 1.574c. The dome's '1.574c' figure is therefore a parameter-fit re-derivation against the disc geometry, not a quotation of Tesla's number. The ~1.57c phase velocity is genuine physics
patches.rationale: EXP-303 PATCH-303-D: ISS-1260 — make explicit that 471,240/299,792=1.5719c ≠ dome's 1.574c; the gap is the post-hoc-fit fingerprint.

patches.find: the coupling constant is undefined. The dome's current live-site
patches.replace: the coupling constant is undefined. Note also that the −10.9 nT numerator is the disturbed-day excursion during the October 2003 G5 Halloween storm referenced above (Dst = −383 nT), not a quiet-day baseline measurement, so any inferred κ is fitted to a single extreme event — its broad applicability is unestablished even if the WIN-013/014 nulls did not already void the ratio. The dome's current live-site
patches.rationale: EXP-303 PATCH-303-E: ISS-1187 — add disturbed-day caveat that raw-text WIN-010 concedes but live WIN-012 did not. Pre-empts defender pivot to storm-day κ scope.
WIN-013.c5 H4: TLDR understates upper bound. detail_evidence cites 5–15 nT (Hvoždara reports up to 12–15 nT). TLDR should match detail.
